mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 compared to individual series over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and business investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during Fed tightening periods or shows zero autocorrelation in 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings above 250 on the CPI index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market prices in inflation expectations immediately, resulting in zero predictive alpha post-threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from equity returns, yielding a rolling Rank IC below 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad macroeconomic momentum, reinforcing earnings growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between GDP and retail sales breaks the predictive relationship, causing signal accuracy to drop below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 105 index threshold signals supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Market anticipates the threshold breach, resulting in no significant return differential post-crossing.
